TextENGNew: ``...Ah, I'll take this one, please.``
Spotting a single remaining copy of a magazine on the shop counter, I plucked it out and paid at the register.
I tucked the change and receipt into my wallet, then headed toward the ticket gate while flipping through my new purchase.
``I missed buying this month's issue and had pretty much given up on it...@k@vS20/35/VTK2_nagisa2004. Finding one in a place like this -- maybe this is my lucky day.``
Murmuring that, I leafed through the magazine I always subscribed to and found my favorite feature section.
I know people would say it's bad manners to read while walking... but there was just one page I couldn't wait to check.
``Let's see, this week's fortune... oh?@k@vS20/35/VTK2_nagisa2006. Thank goodness...``

My heart pounding with a mix of anxiety and anticipation, I turned to that page... and when I saw what was written under my zodiac sign in the horoscope column, I couldn't help but cheer and do a little skip.
The horoscope gave me a 90% luck rating.
Whether by coincidence or genuine good fortune... my lucky item happened to be something already inside my bag.
changeReason: Old translates ラッキーアイテム as 'good luck charm' which implies a talisman, while new correctly renders it as 'lucky item' (a horoscope recommendation); also old says 'Is it coincidence or am I lucky' (self-directed question) while new says 'Whether by coincidence or genuine good fortune' (reflective statement).

TextENGNew: The auspicious reading lifted my spirits somehow.
Fortune-telling might be nothing but nonsense, for all I know.
...But getting such a lucky reading right before meeting Reina filled me with happiness, and I felt courage welling up inside me more and more.
...I took the small package out of my bag and looked at it.
Nestled in a cute gift bag, it was a token of affection I had bought to give Reina when we met... if we managed to make up.
changeReason: Old implies the gift serves dual purposes of showing care and wanting to reconcile, while new adds a conditional 'if we managed to make up' suggesting the gift would only be given upon successful reconciliation, which shifts the narrator's certainty about the outcome.

TextENGNew: ...Will she be happy with it, I wonder?
More importantly... will I even be able to hand it to her?
Anxiety and fear... mixed with a touch of anger and sorrow, these feelings still tangled into hesitation and doubt... trying to drag my heart down into darkness.
But... I'm going.
I... am absolutely going to meet Reina.
Repeating that to myself again and again to rally my timid heart, I forced my faltering legs forward, one step at a time.
...It'll be fine.
Even the horoscope says luck is on my side.
And besides, there was that phone call from Reina just the other day...
I couldn't make out her actual words, but her voice and its resonance had conveyed her feelings to me, hadn't they?
changeReason: Old says 'I could only hear her words' implying the words were audible, while new says 'I couldn't make out her actual words' which is the opposite meaning; the Japanese 言葉として聞くことはできなかった means she could NOT hear the words, so new is more accurate.

TextENGNew: ``I want to see you again``...
``...@k@vS20/35/VTK2_nagisa2008. All right.``
With a soft rallying cry, I lifted my head, tucked the magazine into my bag, and set off toward the ticket gate.
If I go back to Oomaki, I can catch a highway bus from there.
changeReason: The romanization of the place name 大牧 differs: old uses 'Oumaki' while new uses 'Oomaki', which could affect reader recognition of the location.

TextENGNew: Just a few hours on that, and I'll arrive.
After that, I'll meet Reina... and then...
Just then, I saw a ticket slip out of the pocket of the man walking ahead of me.
But he didn't seem to notice and kept walking, moving farther and farther ahead without stopping.
I hesitated for a moment, then hurried over and picked it up. When I caught up right behind him, I mustered my nervous courage and called out.
``...Excuse me.@k@vS20/35/VTK2_nagisa2011. You dropped this...?``
``Hm?@k@vS20/32/VTK2_hanada2002. Oh, thanks.@k@vS20/32/VTK2_hanada2003. ...Wait.``
changeReason: Old translates あれ as 'Huh?' (confusion/surprise), while new uses 'Wait' (realization), giving a different impression of the character's reaction upon seeing Nagisa's face.

TextENGNew: The man took the ticket with a smile, but when he saw my face, a look of mild surprise crossed his features.
...I don't recognize him.
Have we met somewhere before?
``...?@k@vS20/35/VTK2_nagisa2013. Um, is something the matter?``
``No... could you possibly be Nagisa Ozaki-san?``
``?@k@vS20/35/VTK2_nagisa2015. Y-Yes, that's me...``
Hearing my name spoken so suddenly, I instinctively tensed up.
How does this man know my name...?

``Oh! I'm sorry for being so abrupt.@k@vS20/32/VTK2_hanada2006. The truth is, I work under Inspector Minai... and there was something I needed to tell you, so I was actually on my way to visit your home.``
changeReason: Old translates 南井警部 as 'Chief Inspector Minai' while new uses 'Inspector Minai'; 警部 (keibu) is 'Inspector' not 'Chief Inspector' (which would be 警部長), so the rank is different.

TextENGNew: ``Under Minai-san...?@k@vS20/35/VTK2_nagisa2017. Does that mean you're a detective?``
``Yes, that's right.@k@vS20/32/VTK2_hanada2008. ...Here, my credentials.@k@vS20/32/VTK2_hanada2009. Does that put your mind at ease?``
changeReason: Old translates 手帳 as 'badge' while new uses 'credentials'; the Japanese 手帳 refers to a police ID notebook/holder, not a badge, so new is more accurate. Also 'You can trust me, all right?' vs 'Does that put your mind at ease?' conveys different tone.

TextENGNew: Perhaps being considerate of the crowded station, the man discreetly pulled a black badge holder from his breast pocket and showed it to me where no one else could see.
That was definitely the police emblem.
Combined with the reassurance that he knew Minai-san's name, I dropped my guard and turned to face him.
``So... did Minai-san have something for me?``

``You called the inspector the other day, didn't you?@k@vS20/32/VTK2_hanada2011. When she heard about it, she sent me to come get you.@k@vS20/32/VTK2_hanada2012. She said she wanted to speak with you in person, so I'm afraid I don't know the details.``
changeReason: Old says 'You left a message for her' implying an answering machine/voicemail, while new says 'You called the inspector' which just means a phone call; the Japanese お電話されました simply means 'you called', so the old adds an implication not present in the original.

TextENGNew: ``Minai-san... wants to talk to me?@k@vS20/35/VTK2_nagisa2020. Um...``
Unsure what to do, I hesitated to answer.
I was supposed to take a bus after this... and head to Hinamizawa today.
But Minai-san was always so busy -- it would be wrong to refuse when she had gone out of her way to make time for me.
And if I let this chance pass, it would probably be a long time before I could see her again...
...It can't be helped.
I had actually planned to arrive early and settle my nerves before the appointment, but I'll make a detour.
Besides, Minai-san might be able to give me good advice before I meet Reina.
And if I end up running late, maybe I can ask them to drive me there in a patrol car... just kidding.
``All right.@k@vS20/35/VTK2_nagisa2023. ...But I have somewhere to be afterward, so would it be okay if I only stay until then?``
``Of course, that's perfectly fine.@k@vS20/32/VTK2_hanada2014. Thank you for your cooperation... shall we go?``
